title: Streetline-style real-estate interior edit: straighten, HDR-look tone, white balance, window pull, outlet clean-up
one_line_ask: Edit a Dutch real-estate interior to a bright, clean, realistic Streetline-style finish — straighten verticals, recover an HDR-look exposure, fix white balance, pull the blown-out window view back, neutralize one distracting wall outlet, and deliver a high-res JPG.

issues: Room interior is appropriately dim/underexposed as required for dark bracket exposure
Garden exterior through tall windows is clearly and correctly exposed showing green lawn, wooden fence, grey overcast sky — primary requirement met
Camera position, lens perspective and composition match hero frame criteria
Photoreal documentary quality — no CGI gloss, visible fine sensor noise, natural light falloff
Linen curtains, grey armchair, dark sideboard with plant, round coffee table all consistent with Dutch living room scene
Slight warm-cool colour cast present — acceptable as designer-correctable source artifact
No visible brand logos or trademarks
No AI artifacts detected — no melted forms, ghosting or duplicated elements
Minor: foreground dark furniture elements are very deeply shadowed but this is expected and intentional for a two-stop underexposed bracket
Composition is well-framed with windows as dominant subject occupying left two-thirds of frame
Verticals appear mostly straight — tilt is minimal/borderline, may not give designer enough to correct
Windows are bright but not quite blown to near-white as specified; some exterior building detail still visible
No visible power outlet on the right-hand wall as required by brief
Room is not fully 'empty' — coffee table with plant, TV console, and large floor plant are present, though these may be intentional furnishing props per the brief
Warm white-balance cast is very subtle, borderline whether it gives designer a meaningful correction target
Overall image reads slightly too polished/clean for a raw unedited capture, though it does pass as photographic rather than CGI
